decision_point: In an adult with isolated medial compartment knee osteoarthritis, is unicompartmental (partial) knee arthroplasty or total knee arthroplasty preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with isolated medial compartment knee osteoarthritis, is unicompartmental (partial) knee arthroplasty or total knee arthroplasty preferred?
Options:
  1. Partial knee replacement
  2. Total knee replacement
label: patient_dependent
label_rationale: The TOPKAT trial showed similar 10-year clinical outcomes, reoperations, and revisions, but partial knee replacement had fewer complications and was more cost-effective.

reviews.notes: which-option label stratum via llm_haiku — The TOPKAT trial demonstrated equivalent long-term clinical outcomes and revision rates between partial and total knee arthroplasty, with partial KA showing advantages in cost and complications, yet practice remains divided without a famous public debate driving consensus.

auditor reasoning: Fierce AAOS/AAHKS podium debate (not quiet); also strictly gated by ACL integrity/other-compartment disease. | Claude second opinion: Agree. This is one of the loudest AAOS/AAHKS podium fights, and PKR is strictly gated by intact ACL/absence of PF or other-compartment disease.
